title: citrus × nobilis subf. koshotankan
benefits: Citrus × nobilis subf. koshotankan has many benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, which can be seen in its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. It also possesses antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that help protect against infections and cell damage, potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Furthermore, the plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory effects on digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and may even help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. By incorporating Citrus × nobilis subf. koshotankan into one's diet or lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately enhancing their quality of life.
parts: Citrus × nobilis subf. koshotankan has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and peels. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, particularly nobiletin and tangeritin, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of Citrus × nobilis subf. koshotankan are rich in essential oils, including limonene and beta-pinene, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The peels, on the other hand, are a rich source of hesperidin, a flavonoid glycoside with potential anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular benefits.
side_effects: Citrus × nobilis subf. koshotankan can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like hives and itching. In rare cases, excessive consumption may lead to kidney or liver damage due to the plant's high concentration of flavonoids and limonoids. Individuals with pre-existing conditions, such as high blood pressure or kidney disease, may be more susceptible to adverse reactions. Improper use or excessive dosing of this plant can also cause interactions with medications, potentially exacerbating underlying health conditions.
